title_en: Ibn al-Athir: The Historian of Islam's Early Generations
content_en:
Izz al-Din Abu al-Hasan Ali ibn Muhammad ibn Muhammad ibn Abd al-Karim ibn al-Athir al-Jazari (555–630 AH / 1160–1233 CE) was one of the most prolific and important historians in the history of Islamic scholarship. Born in Jazira ibn Umar on the Tigris River in upper Mesopotamia, he and his brothers (including the famous grammarian Majd al-Din ibn al-Athir and the litterateur Diya al-Din ibn al-Athir) formed one of the most remarkable scholarly families in the Islamic world, each achieving distinction in a different field.

Izz al-Din ibn al-Athir spent much of his adult life in Mosul, then a major center of Islamic culture under the Zengid dynasty, and had extensive contact with Saladin's court in Syria and Egypt. He witnessed the Crusades from the Muslim side and the beginning of the Mongol expansion from the far East — events he would chronicle in his major works. His personal experience of the turbulent events of his era informed his historical writing with a quality of lived witness that distinguishes it from purely bookish compilation.

His scholarly output was remarkable for its scope and quality. Al-Kamil fi al-Tarikh (The Complete in History), his universal history in thirteen volumes, is one of the greatest works of Islamic historiography and the primary source for the history of the Islamic world in the 5th–6th centuries AH. Usd al-Ghabah fi Ma'rifat as-Sahaba (Lions of the Forest in Knowing the Companions) is his major contribution to Islamic biographical literature. His Nihayat al-Gharib, a dictionary of rare hadith terminology, demonstrates his command of hadith sciences.

Usd al-Ghabah was conceived as a supplement and completion of Ibn Abd al-Barr's Al-Isti'ab, covering companions that the earlier work had missed or treated inadequately. The title — 'Lions of the Forest' — is a poetic reference to the companions' heroic qualities, with the 'forest' being the world in which these lions moved and left their mark. The work's relationship to Al-Isti'ab makes them natural companions, and they are often published and studied together.
title_id: Ibnu al-Atsir: Sejarawan Generasi-generasi Awal Islam
content_id:
Izz al-Din Abu al-Hasan Ali ibn Muhammad ibn Muhammad ibn Abd al-Karim ibn al-Atsir al-Jazari (555-630 H / 1160-1233 M) adalah salah satu sejarawan paling produktif dan paling penting dalam sejarah Islam. Lahir di wilayah Jazira, beliau menghabiskan sebagian besar hidupnya di Mosul dan menjadi ulama yang menguasai berbagai disiplin ilmu: hadis, tafsir, fikih, sejarah, dan bahasa Arab.

Di antara karya-karya besarnya: Al-Kamil fi at-Tarikh (ensiklopedia sejarah Islam yang sangat komprehensif), Jami' al-Ushul fi Ahadits al-Rasul (kompilasi hadis dari enam kitab hadis utama), dan Usd al-Ghabah fi Ma'rifat as-Shahabah (kamus biografi para Sahabat Nabi ﷺ). Ketiganya menjadi referensi standar dalam bidangnya masing-masing hingga hari ini.
content_ar: Izz al-Din Abu al-حسن علي ibn محمد ﷺ ibn محمد ﷺ ibn Abd al-Karim ibn al-Athir al-Jazari (555–630 AH / 1160–1233 CE) was one of the most prolific and المهم historians in the history of Islamic scholarship. Born in Jazira ibn عمر on the Tigris River in upper Mesopotamia, he and his brothers (بما في ذلك the famous grammarian Majd al-Din ibn al-Athir and the litterateur Diya al-Din ibn al-Athir) formed one of the most remarkable العلمي families in the Islamic الدنيا, each achieving distinction in a different field. Izz al-Din ibn al-Athir spent much of his adult الحياة in Mosul, then a major center of Islamic culture under the Zengid dynasty, and had extensive contact with Saladin's court in Syria and Egypt. He witnessed the Crusades from the مسلم side and the beginning of the Mongol expansion from the far East — events he would chronicle in his major works. His personal experience of the turbulent events of his era informed his historical writing with a quality of lived witness that distinguishes it from purely bookish compilation. His العلمي output was remarkable for its scope and quality. Al-Kamil fi al-Tarikh (The Complete in History), his universal history in thirteen volumes, is one of the greatest works of Islamic historiography and the الأساسي source for the history of the Islamic الدنيا in the 5th–6th centuries AH. Usd al-Ghabah fi Ma'rifat as-الصحابة (Lions of the Forest in Knowing the الصحابة) is his major contribution to Islamic biographical literature. His Nihayat al-Gharib, a dictionary of rare الحديث terminology, demonstrates his command of الحديث sciences. Usd al-Ghabah was conceived as a supplement and completion of Ibn Abd al-Barr's Al-Isti'ab, covering الصحابة that the earlier work had missed or treated inadequately. The title — 'Lions of the Forest' — is a poetic reference to the الصحابة' heroic qualities, with the 'forest' being the الدنيا in which these lions moved and left their mark. The work's relationship to Al-Isti'ab makes them natural الصحابة, and they are often published and studied together.
